generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T DATURA FEROX. Datura ferox, Linn. Natural order , Ranunculaceæ (Chinese Datura, probably the most poisonous species). Preparation , Trituration of the seeds. Authority. 1 , Waitz Prakt. Beob. üb. Java. Arzum., 1829, a girl ate half an unripe fruit (as large as an acorn) mixed with her food. ► After a quarter of an hour she was completely out of her mind, was dizzy, sleepy; at last fell into a sleep, in which the eyes remained more or less open, and if disturbed she sprang up raving, and uttered unintelligible words; if one sang she began to dance in the Javanese fashion, [1] . ► Pulse was slow and full, [1] . ► Mouth very dry, [1] . Buchner's Toxicologie, 1827, states that if the lips are held for a short time to a glass rubbed with a leaf, one is made to rave. Gmelin, Reise, etc., state that beer poisoned with the seeds of this plant causes ravings. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main

keynotes.text: Poisoning with this drug causes raving madness. A girl who ate half an unripe fruit manifested the following: Completely out of her mind in a quarter of an hour, dizzy, sleepy; slept with eyes open; if disturbed, sprang up raving and uttered unintelligible words; if one sang she began to dance in the Javanese fashion. This symptom may prove a keynote in certain cases of mental alienation. Dryness of mouth was also noted in this case, as with Bell. and other Solanaceæ. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main
